notes: With-skill runs achieve 100% pass rate across both evals — every assertion satisfied in both with-scope and cold-start scenarios.

Without-skill runs fail primarily on: technical altitude (schemas in PRD), missing user stories, missing glossary, and missing pipeline integration. The 'write a PRD' task without guidance produces a technical design doc, not a product requirements doc.

Assertion 'quality-gate-ran' passes in both configs (3/4 runs) — baseline Claude does attempt a quality review when asked. However, the baseline quality gate doesn't check glossary conditions or suggest pipeline next steps.
The strongest differentiators are: user stories (0% baseline vs 100% skill), glossary generation (0% vs 100%), and technical altitude (0% vs 100%).
With-skill runs take ~73% longer (414s vs 239s) and use ~74% more tokens (73k vs 42k). The increase comes from deeper interviews (24-27 questions vs 12-14), quality gate, and glossary generation.
Cold start (no scope doc) works cleanly — skill correctly starts with problem/motivation and covers all 9 domains.
Scope-aware start works cleanly — skill correctly skips problem/motivation and references scope findings.
